meta_title: For Freelancers: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Claude Code Skills Setup for SaaS Builders
content.seo_description: Master Claude Code skills to bypass SaaS knowledge gaps. A high-impact guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster than ever.
content.answer_block: Overcome the lack of deep SaaS architecture knowledge by leveraging Claude Code’s autonomous agent capabilities. By integrating Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can translate high-level business requirements into production-ready, scalable infrastructure without needing years of traditional backend engineering experience.
content.intro:
The blank editor is a graveyard for ambitious ideas. You have the client, the vision, and the deadline, but the technical abyss of SaaS architecture—auth flows, database schema normalization, and API middleware—stands between you and the paycheck. You aren't paid to struggle with boilerplate; you are paid to ship.

Today, we bridge that gap. By combining the rapid architectural scaffolding of Antigravity with the autonomous coding power of Claude Code, you transform from a solo freelancer into a technical force. This workflow isn't just about writing code; it’s about commanding an AI-driven engine to build your SaaS infrastructure while you focus on the product-market fit.

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding with Antigravity
steps.description: Generate your core SaaS foundation. Antigravity handles the heavy lifting of project structure, ensuring your directory follows enterprise-grade patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust SaaS boilerplate using Next.js 14, Supabase for auth, and Prisma for ORM, including a standard folder structure for API routes and server actions.

steps.title: Deep-Linking Claude Code to Local Context
steps.description: Establish a persistent connection between Claude Code and your local codebase so the agent understands your specific project constraints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the existing directory structure and create a context map for Claude to understand the relationship between the UI components and server actions.

steps.title: Implementing Secure Authentication Flows
steps.description: Use Claude to wire up your auth provider without manually debugging complex session middleware.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a secure sign-in and sign-up flow using Supabase Auth with server-side protection for sensitive user routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude "Implement secure session management using Supabase and Next.js middleware"

steps.title: Automating Database Schema Migrations
steps.description: Let Claude manage your Prisma schema changes, ensuring your database remains in sync with your evolving SaaS features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a User Subscription model with fields for Stripe customer IDs and plan tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude "Update prisma schema to include subscription models and run migration"

steps.title: Optimizing for Production Performance
steps.description: Use Claude Code to audit your codebase for performance bottlenecks and security vulnerabilities before client handover.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a security and performance audit on the current codebase, focusing on database query efficiency and API route security.
steps.claude_code_command: claude "Audit code for performance, optimize database queries, and fix any security vulnerabilities"

pro_tips: Use 'claude --read-only' on your core config files to prevent the AI from accidentally refactoring your critical auth logic.
Define a custom .claude-ignore file to prevent the agent from indexing large dependency folders or auto-generated cache files.
Always run your Antigravity scaffolding inside a clean git branch so you can diff the changes and learn the architectural patterns being implemented.
Ask Claude to generate unit tests alongside features; it forces the AI to write more modular, testable code in the first pass.
Use the 'claude diff' command to manually review every line of code before accepting, ensuring you maintain full architectural control.
content.next_steps: Deploy your staging environment to Vercel and begin your first round of client feedback cycles.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the code and start dominating the market. With this setup, you have the leverage of a senior engineer at your fingertips. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
